response.justification: Bogdan has a long career spanning geospatial work (GISCollective) and distributed systems (Sociomantic), which are tangentially relevant, but the role's core requirement is expert-level modern C++ or Rust for routing data pipelines — and the candidate's primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. The only C++ experience on record is a National Instruments role from 2000–2001 and a bachelor thesis project, both over two decades old and at small scale. There is no evidence of routing tile generation, navigation data pipelines, or real-time traffic integration, making the gap too wide for a mid-senior IC role.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain exposure through GISCollective, an open-source event-driven geo-data platform used in production
Distributed systems background at Sociomantic handling millions of low-latency HTTP requests per second using async I/O
Consistent Linux server experience across multiple roles spanning the full career
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust — the listed skills and all recent work are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, none of which satisfy the primary language requirement
C++ experience is limited to two minor engagements circa 2000–2001, far below the commercial or open-source depth the role requires
Zero experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tiling, map freshness pipelines, or real-time traffic signal integration
